title: batrachium nipponicum var. major
intro: Batrachium nipponicum var. major, also known as the Japanese waterwort or Japanese marshwort, is a plant species that belongs to the family Ranunculaceae. It is a type of aquatic plant that grows in marshy areas and has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in reducing swelling and pain, as well as having antimicrobial properties that can aid in fighting off infections. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions and decoctions, and is often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
